steps.title: Identify the functional distinction between the technologies
steps.content: The decisive clue is what each technology does. From the notes: neuromodulation influences neural activity, while neuroprostheses restore lost sensory, motor, or neural functions. These are fundamentally different purposes. To contrast Thync (neuromodulation) with BrainGate (neuroprostheses), the answer must highlight this functional difference.

steps.title: Apply the distinction to the choices
steps.content: C directly states the contrast: “Neuromodulation technologies influence neural activity, but they don't restore sensory, motor, or neural functions as neuroprostheses do.” This captures exactly what each technology does and why they differ. The other three either misrepresent the technologies (A incorrectly calls Thync's tech a neuroprosthetic; D wrongly describes neuroprostheses as allowing thought-control of external hardware—that is what BCIs do) or fail to explain the functional distinction (B merely names the technology types without showing how they differ). That matches choice C — C is correct.
